mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL indicates persistent inflation that forces discount rate expectations higher, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% when CPIAUCSL rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack reduces consumer spending growth and corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals robust aggregate demand that lifts corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to generate a statistically significant positive return differential with p-value greater than 0.05 over 60 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output that improves short-term market sentiment and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases produce negative or flat 5-day forward returns in greater than 50% of observations during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5 percent raises borrowing costs for corporations and households, suppressing investment and consumption while generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in greater than 65% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative rank IC over any 2-year rolling window or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic activity that increases corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth correlates negatively with 20-day forward returns or produces a rank IC below 0.01 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below the 2017 baseline index level of 100.0, manufacturing contraction signals weakening economic momentum that triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings below 100.0 are followed by positive 5-day returns in greater than 60% of cases, invalidating the contraction signal.
